core_mechanism: Small excitations linearized on a moving background medium obey a relativistic wave equation in an effective curved-spacetime metric; the critical surface where the background flow speed crosses the local signal speed acts as a one-way horizon that mixes positive- and negative-norm modes and emits correlated pairs of quanta with a thermal spectrum, manifesting as long-range partner correlations in the two-point correlation function.
brief_reason: The abstract reports thermal-like emission of phonons from a sonic horizon in a flowing condensate, matching analog-model predictions in the hydrodynamic limit and revealed by peculiar long-range patterns in the density-density correlation function, which is exactly horizon-induced correlated-pair creation on an effective metric.
